title: Repair stale recurring briefing health check

source_signals.signal: briefing health artifact stale beyond configured cadence

questions.question: Does this proposal preserve proposal-only status until an authorized build review exists?
questions.why_it_matters: A scout may detect work, not approve work.

questions.question: Which current local health signal proves the briefing check is stale rather than merely quiet?
questions.why_it_matters: Build candidates should rest on current metadata, not vibes.

questions.question: Could the repair touch external delivery, scheduler activation, credentials, or source-of-truth records?
questions.why_it_matters: Protected surfaces require approval boundaries before implementation.

questions.question: Which stale and fresh fixtures prove the health checker fails loud and clears cleanly?
questions.why_it_matters: Trust state needs deterministic evidence, not a builder summary.

assumptions: The fixture uses synthetic health metadata and does not represent a live scheduler row.
retrieval_or_probe_plan: Compare stale and fresh synthetic fixtures
missing_evidence: Current scheduler status and validator output are required before build planning in a real system.
accepted_proxies: Synthetic stale/fresh fixture files stand in for live scheduler artifacts in this public example.
questions_intentionally_deferred: Live delivery proof remains deferred until a separate approved activation.
revisit_triggers: The repair requires external send, credential access, service restart, destructive change, or source-of-truth mutation.

mode_outputs.reject_or_plan_recommendation: plan only after bounded approval review

who_benefits: Founder and operator who rely on the recurring briefing
why_now: The latest health metadata is stale and the next briefing depends on fresh source checks.
proposed_scope: Inspect the recurring briefing health contract
verification_ideas: Run health checker on stale and fresh fixtures
Confirm scheduler state was not changed
